message:
fix(ci): strip Windows UNC prefix in JS normalizePath to prevent /?/ corruption

On Windows, canonicalize() produces \\?\C:\... paths. When the JS VFS
normalizePath() replaces backslashes with forward slashes, this becomes
/?/C:/... which is invalid. The host readFileSync callback then fails
with error 123 (ERROR_INVALID_NAME).

Fix normalizePath to detect and strip the corrupted /?/ prefix when
followed by a drive letter (e.g. /?/C:/ → C:/). This single-point fix
protects all callers regardless of whether they strip UNC before passing
cwd to the JS runtime.

Also add strip_unc_prefix to extensions_auto_repair_unit.rs helper as
belt-and-suspenders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
